_mode_3_notes: The bolt-4 SCM scanner does NOT invoke Mode-1 Ivy resolution.
It falls through to Mode-3 default path scan: SHA1 fingerprints of binary files (jar/war/dll/etc).
Each lib/*.jar appears as a flat direct dependency with no transitive structure.
Configuration-mapping and configuration-extends semantics are exercised at lib-population time (we curated the 6 JARs).
The group field on each package records the Ivy configuration the JAR belongs to — compile/runtime/test/provided/master.
Mode-1 testing would require pipeline-mode `mend ua -c whitesource.config`, not SCM integration.
hamcrest-core-1.3.jar is junit's transitive dep; committed to lib/ so Mode-3 can fingerprint it.
warnings: Probe pivoted from Mode-1 (Ivy resolution) to Mode-3 (binary path scan) because the bolt-4 SCM scanner does not honor ant.ivyResolveDependencies=true from repo-root whitesource.config.
JARs were pre-resolved from Maven Central and committed to lib/ to give Mode-3 something to fingerprint.
Configuration-mapping (#6): five distinct conf-mapping syntaxes appear in ivy.xml — compile->default, runtime->compile(*),master(*), test->default, provided->default, *->@.
Configuration-extends (#7): test extends runtime extends compile; Mode-3 flattens the hierarchy — all 6 JARs appear as direct deps with no inherited relationship visible.
hamcrest-core-1.3.jar is junit's transitive dep (pulled in by junit:4.13.2 via POM dep on hamcrest-core:1.3). Committed to lib/ so Mode-3 fingerprints it.

servlet-api:2.5 (provided scope) is isolated: NOT inherited by test/runtime/compile. In Mode-1 it would not appear on the runtime classpath. In Mode-3 it appears as a flat direct dep alongside the rest.
commons-io:2.11.0 uses *->@ (identity mapping) on the master conf: only the artifact itself, no transitive deps. Both behaviors are indistinguishable in Mode-3.
